message:
fix(resource): accept full Linux RLIMIT_* range + diff harness

CONFORMANCE: libc sys/resource.h diff matrix.

Differential harness exposed a real conformance defect: getrlimit
(and setrlimit + prlimit + ulimit by transitive logic) rejected
RLIMIT_RSS, RLIMIT_NPROC, RLIMIT_MEMLOCK, RLIMIT_LOCKS,
RLIMIT_SIGPENDING, RLIMIT_MSGQUEUE, RLIMIT_NICE, RLIMIT_RTPRIO, and
RLIMIT_RTTIME with EINVAL because the core's `valid_resource()`
allowlist enumerated only a sparse subset (CPU/FSIZE/DATA/STACK/CORE/
NOFILE/AS) — leaving 9 of the 16 standard Linux rlimits unreachable.

Glibc and the host kernel accept and honor every value in the
contiguous 0..=15 (RLIMIT_CPU..RLIMIT_RTTIME) range. Any program that
asked our libc for, e.g., the per-user process limit or queued-signal
budget got EINVAL where they expected the kernel value.

Fix: extend the constants in frankenlibc_core::resource to declare
all 11 Linux rlimits, replace the sparse `matches!` allowlist with a
contiguous range check `(RLIMIT_CPU..=RLIMIT_MAX_VALID).contains(&r)`,
and update the in-source unit test to assert each value.

Companion harness:
- getrlimit_cases: 8 named resources + invalid_resource probe (rc/errno)
- setrlimit_roundtrip: cross-impl observe of post-set value
  (FrankenLibC sets, glibc reads + vice versa; restores prior on exit)
- getrusage_self_and_children: rc parity + ru_maxrss with 64 KiB
  drift tolerance; invalid_who errno match
- getpriority_self: PRIO_PROCESS/0 rc + errno parity

Full abi suite: 3114/3114 pass across 75 suites; core resource lib
tests 2/2 pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
